mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index signal deepening manufacturing weakness that translates into lower corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of months when INDPRO records three consecutive monthly declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.5%, it triggers a discrete upward revision of recession probability by market participants, causing rapid risk-off repositioning that generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: After UNRATE exceeds 6.5%, positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances over a five-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts confirm a broad-based collapse in consumer and housing demand, jointly amplifying recession severity and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
candidate_trigger: month-over-month decline in both RSAFS and HOUST
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month, positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur more than 45% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: During the zero lower bound regime, month-over-month deceleration in the consumer price index raises ex-post real interest rates and increases the real debt burden, depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25%, decelerating CPIAUCSL is followed by positive average 60-day returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A reduction in the federal funds rate lowers short-term borrowing costs for financial intermediaries, triggering a rapid repricing of near-term liquidity risk and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Federal funds rate cuts are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of months.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 650 thousand units, it signals a frozen residential construction sector that drags down construction employment and aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts below 650 thousand are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in monthly retail sales indicates resilient consumer spending that supports corporate revenue forecasts and margins, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Months with RSAFS growth are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP confirms an economy-wide demand shortfall that forces negative earnings revisions across sectors, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Quarters with negative GDPC1 growth are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.
